name: Trending Threads
description: Displays the most active threads in a dedicated sidebar section based on recency-weighted activity scores.

settings_schema.header: Configure how trending threads are calculated and displayed.
settings_schema.footer: Changes take effect after saving and may require up to one refresh interval to appear.

settings.help_text: Determines which channels are included in trending calculations. 'server' includes all public channels; 'channels' allows you to specify a comma-separated list of channel IDs.
Only used when Scope is set to 'channels'. Enter a comma-separated list of channel IDs (e.g., channelid1,channelid2,channelid3).
How far back to look when calculating trending threads. Only threads with activity within this window are considered.
The maximum number of trending threads to display in the sidebar.
How often the trending threads list is recalculated. Lower values provide more real-time updates but increase server load.
After a new message is posted, wait this many seconds with no further qualifying posts before recalculating trending. Bursts of messages trigger one refresh. Set to 0 to use the default of 2 seconds.
Advanced: Weight factor for replies in the trending score calculation. Higher values favor discussion-heavy threads.
Advanced: Weight factor for reactions in the trending score calculation. Higher values favor reaction-heavy threads.
Advanced: Exponential decay rate for recency weighting. Higher values (e.g., 0.2) favor more recent activity; lower values (e.g., 0.05) give more weight to total activity regardless of age.
